title: gomphandra quadrifida var. ovalifolia
intro: Gomphandra quadrifida var. ovalifolia is a plant species belonging to the Guttiferae family, also known as the tanner's buckeye or gomphandra, a type of evergreen shrub or small tree native to Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains various bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gomphandra quadrifida var. ovalifolia include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory diseases. Traditionally, the plant's bark is prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, although its preparation and dosage may vary depending on the specific herbal practice.
